item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Really sad this isn't longer or with a collaborative testing log. Only flaw, +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I didn’t even realize I was doing that until you mentioned it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Is it cross testing, or will the monkey begin to feel the effects of depression? Or has it been used in testing a amnestic agent or some other foundation tech?

The last bit definitely is not lolFoundation, but it's also a pretty obvious route to take. I've read this kind of mind reveal in other items before. (Hello again, desire camera)

But the tennis ball! So sad, so horrifying! Are all inanimate items cursed with a mind? Or maybe the camera just made that up because that's all it was given to work with.

item.title: Junior Staff Post
item.content:encoded: The image could not be sourced reliably and is assumed not compliant with CC 3.0. As such, it has since been removed.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I liked this. Funny, potencially dangerous, and the more you think about it, creepy (it reads minds and apparently predicts the future). I, unlike many people here, don't see any LOLFoundation, the last log is perfectly plausible, not exaggerated.
+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The piece is light, though the object is serious enough. The Containment is simple as it should be for a Safe object of this kind, focused on the procedures for experimentation. By the way, the object invites to experimentation, since it creates a lot of potential questions, but at the same time is simple enough to test upon.

I do have an issue with the "brittish accent", since it actually leaves it open to a huge variety of accents in the island, but it is quite a minor issue. The recovery paragraph is longer than needed, and breaks the pacing a little. The disassembly of the object could mention if assembling it with spare parts with no previous anomalous effect would still work.

On third test log we have an almost cross-test. Since it doesn't have enough information about the other SCP involved to really establish major concerns, it is a bit more careless than I think they would be. The possibility of this thing reading the future was already given since log 1.

And the fourth is quite more palatable for me. It isn't lolfoundation, is a mistake that could reasonably happen at research. The item have sufficiently known effects to be deemed harmless, so it shouldn't be neither subject to too strict protocols, nor paid enough attention to avoid that sort of thing. On most circumstances, it accidentally recording a researcher wouldn't cause any major concern.

But I do think it lacks something. The logs could be reworked to better explore the limits of this thing. It could have a more relevant (not necessarily more edgy) recovery record. Also, it could feature in a Tale.

For me, an experiment log isn't the best way to handle most of the questions raised by this kind of skip. A tale can go further into exploring side concepts and possibilities, all the while not affecting the main artice itself.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I read all the narration in Morgan Freeman's voice. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I have not watched those, I was not a fan of documentaries until just recently plus English is not my first language. Instead I imagined the voice to be somewhat like the narrator from Stanley Parable.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: +1 at the tennis ball but I did not like the lolFoundation at the end.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
+1.

Honestly I was confused coming to the discussion and finding that people thought it read minds. From my perspective, the bit with the tennis ball was proof that this thing just made shit up, and the lady Doctor overreacted by taking the dubious word of an anomalous voice at face-value.

Actually, I guess it was the monkey thing, with SCP-Blackbox. I tend to just gloss over redacted stuff… hm.

Either way, thoroughly enjoyed! This is my favorite kind of SCP. Humorous, but seriously delivered.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This was excellent overall. +1

However, I have one complaint…the test log was short. Way too short.

Because that test log, really, was awesome.

I wonder if they've tested it on other SCPs? You could do that.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Almost down voted cause this just seems like an anomalous item rather then a main page item, then I got to the tennis ball. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Very good. +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: +1, but would be better with a collab log.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The male still chuckles, giving this yet another upvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
There is no British accent, damn it! What kind of accent does the narrator have? If it's Attenborough or Fry, it's Received Pronunciation, and I think the Foundation would be scientific enough to know that.

I'm going to huffily read it as a Welsh voice unless you at the very least change it to 'English'.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The guy in my head is Stephen Fry. I guess I liked his narration in Little Big Planet and The Hitchhiker's Guide to the Galaxy.
And as the male casually upvotes the article into +100, he fails to notice that he is being narrated by a British man…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: THIS. Why people keeps thinking that 'human reaction' is LOLFoundation, and that 'fun result' is automatically -J?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I very mildly object to the last two tests, not for being lolfoundation (which they aren't IMO), but because they are scientifically unsound risks of cross contamination.

You have a monkey, in the throes of being exposed to SCP-XXX, exposing it to another anomaly mid-test is just begging to invalidate the data from both experiments. Same for the creeper and the object of his desires. Why were they even allowed to play with the anomalous camera while testing something else? Granted, neither of them seem overly professional, but still…

Anyway, poor experimental protocols aside, I liked it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I know I'm behind the times here; but I would like to call attention to this, the most concise handling of "the lolfoundation question" I have yet encountered.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Although I'd admit that I'd love to see more of this, a longer test log might actually hurt the article by pointing it soundly in the direction of "overpowered".

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
God damn am I glad nobody will ever point this at me.

But seriously, this is excellent.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: An enjoyable read, well-executed. I concur with the prospect of a supplement log. Also, this thing has extraordinary implications. I understand cross-testing is frowned upon, but the creativity involved in seeing what some of the other SCPs are thinking seems like too fun and interesting of an opportunity to pass up.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: You do have a point. I still think it's a tad boring though. An SCP that is mostly an SCP because it could possibly leak information about other SCPs and other secrets just doesn't do much for me. I'll change to a neutral vote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I was thinking, "Hey, a camera that adds commentary to movies? That's pretty neat." And then I actually read the logs and I loved them. I agree, this needs more testing; it could really go places. Not to mention, it doesn't just read minds, it predicts the future! I'm surprised more wasn't made of that.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
VPC-WH1YL

I misread that as SPC, and I thought, "Raven, what the hell are you doing? You know better…. Oh." Overall, a good read.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Have this camera and SCP-978 shoot each other at the same time.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This feels perfectly done. Sure the last note feels a little lolFoundationy but it's a great end to the piece. It was already established that it was a security risk, but explaining the invasion of privacy it represents as the very last note is excellent pacing, because that's the most immediately fearsome thing about this device.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Upvoted by the second line of the description. Yes.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is funny. Therefore, it is not lolFoundation.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Humor isn't lolfoundation.

I didn't see anyone trying to use this on 682 before being put on Keter duty, and Dr. Blackbox punching Dr. Censored after finding out that he may be unhealthily obsessed with her is a fairly reasonable reaction, and "administrative leave pending disciplinary action" is a perfectly reasonable punishment for such an action.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This seems incredibly similar to the Desire Camera to my mind. No vote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It seems more like an anomalous item than a full SCP.

…. it could declassify classified information (the experiment with the Rhesus monkey) and can essentially read minds. If anything, that would walk closer to the overpowered line than the "anomalous item" line (and its ambiguous reliability keeps it from crossing into overpowered).

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Oh, I love this one so very much. The whole "narrating life like David Attenborough" gag is one I'm partial to from the get-go, so to see someone turn it into an actual workable SCP with a funny/disturbing narrative thread is a treat. More than just a joke, this also manages to be a very alarmingly powerful/nigh omniscient? SCP without feeling overpowered or too useful (as there is risk inherent in using it to find out shit). Well done. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Adding another instance of the word lolFoundation to this discussion, complete with link to a related forum thread should any newer members be confused by the word that is popping up a lot here.

I've already posted on this before, but… I like this sort of whimsical SCP. Also, as Vincent stated, it could definitely have some darker undertones (is it reading your mind? Does it sometimes purposely say things that will provoke others nearby?)

Personally, when I read this, I hear the voice of that one gentleman who narrates those short BBC nature clips that my biology professor occasionally shares with the class.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's completely impossible to read it without using his voice, isn't it?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Just sayin', I thought lolfoundation was something along the lines of "unrealistic, wasteful, pointless, whacky hi-jinks by dem cuh-razy scippahs", not an altogether fairly realistic and plausible depiction of a grumpy scientist finding out some weird dude was creepin' on her, because the object read his mind and intentions, which is a pretty creepy concept… What else could it know about you? Also, I kept bouncing between Nigel Thornberry and Steve Irwin as I read this, which amused me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Too whimsical and useful for my liking. Last log kills it for me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I hate you for making me upvote lolFoundation.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Meh. It seems more like an anomalous item than a full SCP. Also lolfoundation.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I mostly like this one, but the last log is too lolfoundation for me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
'Wild fantasies' indeed. +1

This idea is so ludicrous, it works. Hell, I wouldn't even call that last bit lolFoundation because that's something I can see legitimately happening.

Imma hop on the 'This Deserves a Supplement Article' bandwagon. The possibilities for both humor and intrigue here are endless.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Upvoted with glee. All of a sudden I feel like I'm neglecting the ping pong ball sitting at the bottom of my desk drawer.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Ahahaha. I love it. I think you should expand it.

And you even pulled off the lolFoundation. A+

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: tee-hee

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Interesting. I think this could actually benefit from a crosstest.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I want a test log! Specifically one with the desire-camera. I want to know what it wants!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Not sure about the bit at the very end, but I enjoy this. I almost wonder if (in-universe) researchers could film SCPs to gain more information on them.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I thought I wouldn't like this at all before the logs. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I still say this is the best thing ever.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
So, it's a video camera that turns everything it records into an Attenborough documentary.

That…is brilliant.
